title: Farmhouse Bed — California King Size
summary: A planked 1x8 headboard and footboard on a 2x4 leg frame, sized for a California king mattress with a joist frame that adjusts for a box spring or not.
description:
The leg design here is different from a 4x4-post farmhouse bed: each leg is built up from an inner and outer 2x4, glued and screwed face to face, which gives a slimmer profile than a solid post while still carrying real load. Keep the top and outside edges of the inner and outer leg pieces flush as you laminate them — that's the reference surface everything else in the headboard registers from.

The joist spacing is a genuine engineering decision, not a fixed number: 12" spacing is shown, but a mattress with no box spring needs support at least every 6" (the standard maximum slat spacing), while a good box spring can get away with wider spacing. Decide this before cutting joists, and remember a California king often runs on two box springs side by side, not one.

tools.note: Countersinks screws into the leg lamination.

materials.name: Pine, 1x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Panel trim — omitted from the source's own shopping list, added here to cover the cut list.

materials.name: Pine, 1x6, 8 ft
materials.note: Footboard top trim — thinner than the headboard's, since the footboard carries less load.

materials.name: Pine, 2x10, 8 ft
materials.note: Side rails — also omitted from the source's own shopping list.

cutList.note: Measure your actual panel width before cutting; approximate.
Adjust to your actual side-rail thickness.
Measure your actual panel width before cutting; approximate.
Spaced every 12" shown — space at least every 6" if not using a box spring.

steps.title: Build the headboard panel front
steps.body: Lay out the panel boards on a clean, level surface. Measure the total assembled length and cut the trim boards to this measurement (approximately 78-1/2"). Fasten the trim to the panel with 1-1/4" nails and glue, attaching from the back to hide nail holes.

steps.title: Build the headboard panel back
steps.body: Fasten the back trim pieces to the headboard the same way, using glue and 2" nails. This side, with visible nail holes, becomes the back of the headboard.

steps.title: Attach the inner legs
steps.body: Predrill and countersink your screws, then attach the inner legs to the headboard panel with 3" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the outer legs
steps.body: Fasten the outer legs to the inner legs with 3" screws and glue, countersinking again, keeping the top and outside edges flush.

steps.title: Cap the headboard
steps.body: Attach the top rail and top trim to the headboard with 3" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the bottom leg piece
steps.body: Measure your actual side rails before cutting this piece — it may need to be shorter or longer depending on their thickness. Attach with 2" screws and glue, countersinking your screws.

steps.title: Build the footboard
steps.body: Assemble the footboard the same way as the headboard, except the footboard leg pieces are all identical in size and there's no separate bottom leg piece.

steps.title: Build the frame and attach the side rails
steps.body: Build the frame with 3" screws and glue. Space the joists every 12" as shown, adding more if you won't use a box spring (standard maximum slat spacing is 6"), or fewer if you have a strong box spring — remember a California king often uses two box springs side by side. Attach the side rails to the frame with front edges flush; the bottom edge of the frame overhangs the rails by 5/8". Adjust the frame height as needed depending on your box-spring situation.

steps.title: Attach the frame to the headboard
steps.body: Predrill and countersink, then attach the side rails to the headboard with 2-1/2" screws — only a few screws are needed per side, since the bottom leg piece carries the weight. Do not use glue on this joint.

steps.title: Attach the frame to the footboard
steps.body:
Fasten the bottom frame joists directly to the footboard panel with 2" screws. Do not use glue.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: California king farmhouse bed with planked headboard and footboard
